name: O.A.S. Staff
description_short: Federal credit union headquartered in Washington, DC serving government employees with $279.9M in assets, checking/savings accounts, and lending services.
description_long:
O.A.S. Staff Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered credit union established in 1962, making it over 60 years old. It is headquartered at 1889 F Street NW in Washington, DC and serves a membership-based model with approximately 8,852 members and 37 employees. The organization maintains Federal Credit Union charter number 15000 and is a member of the Federal Home Loan Bank system.

The credit union offers standard banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and other loan products. Their loan portfolio totals approximately $163.9 million across real estate ($129.7M), unsecured ($12.3M), and other loan categories ($21.8M). They also provide wire transfer services and international remittance capabilities. Membership is based on multiple common bonds, primarily serving federal, state, and local government employees.

O.A.S. Staff operates as a specialized government employee credit union with deep roots in the Washington, DC government community. Their membership base is restricted to government employees, which differentiates them from general-purpose credit unions and creates a focused, mission-driven lending environment. The organization is not designated as a low-income credit union, indicating it serves moderate to higher-income government workers.

Financial performance shows mixed indicators: as of December 2024, the credit union reported total assets of $279.9 million with $262.3 million in member deposits. However, the most recent financial statement shows a net loss of $47,005, suggesting operational challenges. The organization maintains adequate capital reserves and is properly insured through the National Credit Union Share Insurance Fund. For government employees seeking credit union banking services, this institution offers stability and community focus, though prospective members should verify membership eligibility based on employment. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Money market and other share products
Real estate loans (mortgages and home loans)
pros: 65+ year track record serving government employees since 1962
FHLB member status providing access to wholesale funding and lending products
Federal insurance protection through NCUSIF for all member deposits
Substantial asset base of $279.9 million supporting lending capacity
Offers wire transfers and international remittance services
Only 1 branch location in Washington, DC with dedicated staff of 37 employees
Lower member base (8,852) allows for personalized service vs. large banks
cons: Net loss of $47,005 in most recent reporting period indicates operational strain
Single branch location limits accessibility for members outside Washington, DC area
Membership restricted to government employees—not open to general public
Negative other reserves of $6.9 million suggests balance sheet challenges
Loan loss allowance of only $546K against $163.9M loan portfolio may be understated
best_for: Federal, state, and local government employees seeking credit union banking
Washington, DC-based government workers needing in-person banking services
Government employees wanting membership-based financial institution with decades of history
Members needing international remittance or wire transfer capabilities
diagnosis: O.A.S. Staff Federal Credit Union is best for government employees in the Washington, DC area seeking traditional credit union banking with 65+ years of institutional history. However, prospective members should be aware of recent operating losses and verify membership eligibility based on government employment status before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
